name: realtime
context: Supabase Realtime — subscribe to Postgres changes, broadcast messages, and track user presence via WebSocket
info: Supabase Realtime is an Elixir-based WebSocket server that provides three features: Postgres Changes (listen for INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE on tables), Broadcast (send messages to all clients in a channel), and Presence (track which users are online with shared state). The client SDK uses `supabase.channel('name')` to create channels, then chains `.on()` listeners for each feature. Channels are multiplexed over a single WebSocket connection. Postgres Changes require the `supabase_realtime` publication on the target tables (`ALTER PUBLICATION supabase_realtime ADD TABLE my_table`). RLS policies apply to Postgres Changes — users only receive changes for rows they can SELECT. Broadcast and Presence do not go through Postgres and have no RLS filtering.

sections.title: Postgres Changes
sections.body: Listen for INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE on tables. Requires adding the table to `supabase_realtime` publication via `ALTER PUBLICATION`. Events include `payload.new` (the new row) and `payload.old` (the old row, for UPDATE/DELETE). Filter by column with `filter: 'col=eq.value'`. RLS policies apply — users only see changes they could SELECT.

sections.title: Broadcast messages
sections.body: Send ephemeral messages to all clients in a channel. No Postgres, no persistence, no RLS. Use `channel.send({ type: 'broadcast', event: 'name', payload: data })`. Subscribe with `.on('broadcast', { event: 'name' }, callback)`. Ideal for cursor positions, typing indicators, and game state.

sections.title: Presence tracking
sections.body: Track online users with `channel.track(state)`. Listen for sync/join/leave events. `presenceState()` returns all tracked users. State is per-key (typically user ID). Users automatically leave when they disconnect. Ideal for online indicators, collaborative editing, and typing indicators.
